{{!--
Wiki page: OpenRailwayMap/Tagging_in_France
Last edited: 2026-06-09 22:47:04 UTC
Last editor: Noeld
Last edit comment: Unify "Block System Identification Plate" (Plaque de Cantonnement) and rewrite some sentences in description
Backup date: 2026-06-11 00:29:02 UTC
Source URL: https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/OpenRailwayMap/Tagging_in_France
--}}

{{languages}}
{{WIP}}

This section describes the tagging scheme specific to French railway signalling, based on the [[Tag:railway%3Dsignal|international railway signal]] tagging model.

== JOSM Presets ==
Dedicated [https://josm.openstreetmap.de/wiki/Presets JOSM presets] are available for download directly from the application to simplify the mapping of [[#Tagging|French railway signalling]] according to these specifications. Please see [https://noeldev.github.io/FrenchRailwaySignalling/ French railway signalling JOSM Presets] for more information.

==Tagging==

All railway signals must include the following tags:

* {{Tag|railway|signal}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:direction||({{TagValue|railway:signal:direction|forward}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:direction|backward}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:direction|both}})}} Direction of the signal relative to the direction of the way.
* {{Tag|railway:signal:position||({{TagValue|railway:signal:position|left}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:position|right}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:position|bridge}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:position|catenary_mast}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:position|ground}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:position|in_track}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:position|overhead}})}} Lateral or vertical position of the signal relative to the track.
* {{Tag|railway:position:exact||365.913}} where ''365.913'' is an example. Position of the signal along the track. This corresponds to the kilometric point (PK) of the signal installation, measured from the beginning of the line.

* {{TagKey|railway:signal:main}}''':ref'''=123456 where '''main''' is an example of one of the [https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Tag:railway%3Dsignal#List_of_signal_categories signal categories] and ''123456'' is an example of an '''idreseau''' identifier provided by [https://www.sncf-reseau.com/ SNCF Réseau]. One identifier is used per category and per physical signal, board, or sign at the node.

Optional tags:
* {{Tag|ref|}}: identifier shown on the marker plate ('''plaque de repérage''') when present.


===Stop signals===

====Carré====

An absolute stop signal for normal train operations, typically found on main lines or at stations. The signal displays two steady red lights, arranged either vertically or horizontally. It is equipped with a clearing light (French: '''œilleton'''), which is illuminated in all states except when the signal is closed (i.e. displaying the {{TagValue|railway:signal:main:states|FR:C}} state). The signal also features an ''identification plate'' (French: '''Plaque d'Identification''') bearing the inscription '''Nf''' in white letters on a black background.

The signal may also be equipped with a ''marker plate'' (French: '''Plaque de Repérage''') and/or a ''block system identification plate'' (French: '''plaque de cantonnement''') indicating the ''block system'' beyond the signal. The inscriptions on these plates are written in black letters on a white background.

[[File:Carre_C_BAL.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:main|FR:C}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:main:form|light}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:main:shape||({{TagValue|railway:signal:main:shape|FR:C}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:main:shape|FR:F}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:main:shape|FR:H}})}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:main:states|}} (→ [[FR:Key:railway:signal:main:states|available states]], separated by <code>;</code>)

Optional tags:

'''Features'''
* {{Tag|railway:signal:main:clearing_light|no}} (if not equipped with a clearing light)

'''Plates'''
<!-- Plaque de cantonnement --> 
* {{Tag|railway:signal:main:plate||({{TagValue|railway:signal:main:plate|FR:BM}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:main:plate|FR:PR}})}} (block system identification plate)
* {{Tag|railway:signal:main:plate:caption|}} (text as shown on the ''block system identification plate'')

=====Carré (dwarf)=====

[[File:Signal_Carre_reduit.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:main|FR:C}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:main:form|light}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:main:height|dwarf}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:main:states||}} (→ [[FR:Key:railway:signal:main:states|available states]], separated by <code>;</code>)

Optional tags:
* {{Tag|railway:signal:distant:arrangement||({{TagValue|railway:signal:distant:arrangement|horizontal}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:distant:arrangement|vertical}})}} (signal arrangement, horizontal by default)

=====Carré (mechanical)=====

[[File:CarreMecanique.svg|40px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:main|FR:C}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:main:form|sign}}

=====Carré announcement=====

[[File:Pancarte_à_distance_Carré.svg|40px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:distant|FR:CARRE_A}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:distant:form|sign}}

Optional tags:
* {{Tag|railway:signal:distant:caption|}} (text as shown on the sign)

====Carré violet====

An absolute stop signal, similar to the [[#Carré|Carré signal]], specifically used to control train movements during shunting operations, on service tracks, or during transitions of traffic to and from main lines. The signal displays a single steady purple light and is also equipped with an identification plate bearing the inscription '''Nf''' in white letters on a black background.

Like the Carré signal, this signal may be equipped with a marker plate and/or a block plate with inscriptions in black letters on a white background.

[[File:CV_C_BAL.svg|60px]]
[[File:CV_A_BAL.svg|60px]]
[[File:CV_Sol.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:main|FR:CV}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:main:form|light}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:main:shape||({{TagValue|railway:signal:main:shape|FR:A}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:main:shape|FR:C}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:main:shape|FR:F}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:main:shape|FR:H}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:main:shape|FR:K}})}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:main:states|}} (→ [[FR:Key:railway:signal:main:states|available states]], separated by <code>;</code>)

Optional tags:

<!-- Plaque de Cantonnement -->
* {{Tag|railway:signal:main:plate||({{TagValue|railway:signal:main:plate|FR:BM}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:main:plate|FR:PR}})}} (block system identification plate)
* {{Tag|railway:signal:main:plate:caption|}} (text as shown on the ''block system identification plate'')

=====Carré violet (mechanical)=====

[[File:CarreVM.svg|40px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:main|FR:CV}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:main:form|sign}}

====Sémaphore====

A stop signal that can be passed under certain conditions. The signal may display either a steady or flashing red light. It is also equipped with an '''Identification Plate''' featuring one of the following inscriptions in white letters on a black background: 
* '''F''' (for ''Franchissable'', indicating a block of [https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Block_automatique_lumineux Block Automatique Lumineux])
* '''BM''' (indicating a block of [https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Block_manuel Block Manuel])
* '''PR''' (indicating a block of [https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Block_automatique_%C3%A0_permissivit%C3%A9_restreinte Block Automatique à Permissivité Restreinte])

The actual inscription is specified with the {{TagKey|railway:signal:main:plate}} tag.

[[File:Semaphore_F_BAL_F.svg|60px]]
[[File:Semaphore_A_BAL_F.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:main|FR:S}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:main:form|light}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:main:shape||({{TagValue|railway:signal:main:shape|FR:A}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:main:shape|FR:C}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:main:shape|FR:F}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:main:shape|FR:H}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:main:shape|FR:K}})}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:main:states|}} (→ [[FR:Key:railway:signal:main:states|available states]], separated by <code>;</code>)

Optional tags:
* {{Tag|railway:signal:main:plate||
({{TagValue|railway:signal:main:plate|FR:F}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:main:plate|FR:PR}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:main:plate|FR:BM}})}} (identification plate)

=====Sémaphore (mechanical)=====

[[File:SemaphoreM.PNG|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:main|FR:S}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:main:form|sign}}

====Guidon d'arrêt====

A stop signal that requires the driver to come to a complete stop before the signal. When in the open position, the signal does not display any specific indication other than the red stripe being extinguished. It is equipped with an identification plate with the inscription '''GA''' and may also have a marker plate, both featuring inscriptions in black letters on a white background.

[[File:Guidon_d'Arrêt.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:main|FR:GA}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:main:form|light}}

====Yellow light band====

In some stations, the entrance [[#Carré|Carré signal]] may be equipped with a horizontal yellow light band (French: '''Bande jaune lumineuse horizontale'''). This band is lit in addition to the [[Tag:railway:signal:main:states=FR:A|Caution]] ('''Avertissement''') light. It instructs the driver to be prepared to stop within a short distance, either due to the presence of vehicles or the closure of an intermediate stop signal. 

A [[#TVM_stop_marker|TVM stop marker]] may also be equipped with a yellow light band.

[[File:Bande_Jaune.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:short_route|FR:yellow_band}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:short_route:form|light}}

===Distant signals===

====Disque====

A '''Disque''' signal instructs the driver to proceed at sight ('''Marche à Vue''') as soon as possible or to stop before the first set of points (switch or crossing) protected by the signal. It is equipped with an identification plate bearing the inscription '''D''' in black letters on a white background.

[[File:Disque_BAPR_BM.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:distant|FR:D}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:distant:form|light}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:distant:states|}} (→ [[FR:Key:railway:signal:distant:states|available states]], separated by <code>;</code>)

=====Disque (mechanical)=====

[[File:DisqueMecanique.svg|40px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:distant|FR:D}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:distant:form|sign}}

====Avertissement====

An '''Avertissement''' (Caution) signal indicates the approach to a closed [[#Sémaphore|sémaphore]] of [https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Block_automatique_%C3%A0_permissivit%C3%A9_restreinte Block Automatique à Permissivité Restreinte] (BAPR). It is equipped with an identification plate bearing the inscription '''A''' in black letters on a white background.

[[File:Av_R_BAPR_BM.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:distant|FR:A}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:distant:form|light}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:distant:states|}} (→ [[FR:Key:railway:signal:distant:states|available states]], separated by <code>;</code>)

=====Avertissement (mechanical)=====

[[File:AvertissementMecanique.svg|40px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:distant|FR:A}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:distant:form|sign}}

===Disabled signal===

Signals not currently in use (e.g. deactivated or out of service) are marked with a white Saint Andrew's cross.

[[File:PermCancelled_H_BAL.svg|60px]]

Use one of the following tags depending on the signal type (main or distant):

* {{Tag|railway:signal:main:deactivated|yes}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:distant:deactivated|yes}}

===Combined signals===

In some cases, [[#Shunting_boards|shunting]] and [[#Wrong-road_signalling|wrong-road]] boards may be installed together in a double light box.

[[File:D_TECS_box.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:combined|FR:light_box}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:combined:form|light}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:combined:shape|double}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:combined:left||({{TagValue|railway:signal:combined:left|FR:D}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:combined:left|FR:G}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:combined:left|FR:TECS}})}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:combined:right||({{TagValue|railway:signal:combined:right|FR:D}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:combined:right|FR:TECS}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:combined:right|FR:TSCS}})}}

===Permanent speed limits===

Permanent speed restrictions are signaled:
* either by [[#Ralentissement_30|Ralentissement 30]] (or 60) and [[#Rappel_30|Rappel 30]] (or 60) signals,
* or by ''speed limit indicators'' known as '''Tableaux Indicateurs de Vitesse limite''' ('''TIV'''). TIVs may be [[#Switchable_speed_indicators|switchable]], [[#Fixed_speed_indicators|fixed]] or [[#Pentagonal_speed_indicators|pentagonal]].

====Mechanical speed indicators====

=====Ralentissement 30=====

[[File:RalentissementM.svg|40px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit|FR:R30}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit:form|sign}}

=====Rappel 30=====

Rappel de ralentissement 30.

[[File:RappelRalentissementM.svg|40px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit|FR:RR30}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit:form|sign}}

====Switchable speed indicators====

Points that can only be crossed at reduced speed in the thrown direction (approximately 60 km/h) are typically preceded by two ''switchable speed indicators'': a diamond-shaped '''TIV à Distance''' (TIV-D) followed by a ''speed limit reminder'' called '''TIV de Rappel''' (TIV-R).

These boards can be luminous or mechanical; when open, they present a continuous white vertical band.

=====Switchable TIV-D=====

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center"
!Mechanical!!Luminous
|-
|[[File:TIV_D_MOB_100.svg|60px]]||[[File:TIV_Mobile_100.svg|60px]]
[[File:TIV_Mobile_O.svg|60px]]
|}

* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant|FR:speed_indicator}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant:switchable|FR:TIV-D}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant:switchable:form||
({{TagValue|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant:switchable:form|sign}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant:switchable:form|light}})}} (sign=mechanical; light=luminous box)
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant:switchable:speed|}} (speed as displayed on the signal, e.g. 100)

=====Switchable TIV-R=====

The '''TIV-R''' is always paired with the [[#Carré|Carré]] signal preceding the point.

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center"
!Mechanical!!Luminous
|-
| [[File:TIV_R_MOB_60.svg|60px]] || [[File:TIV_Rappel_Lumineux_60.svg|60px]]
[[File:TIV_Rappel_Lumineux_O.svg|60px]]
|}

* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit|FR:speed_indicator}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit:switchable|FR:TIV-R}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit:switchable:form||
({{TagValue|railway:signal:speed_limit:switchable:form|sign}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:speed_limit:switchable:form|light}})}} (sign=mechanical; light=luminous)
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit:switchable:speed|}} (speed as displayed on the signal, e.g. 60)

====Chevron pointe en bas====

If necessary, the location of the point (or the first point in the case of successive points) is identified by a downward-pointing chevron.

In some cases, particularly at diverging exits from certain station tracks, a 30 km/h speed reminder may apply to a trailing point rather than to a facing point. In such cases, the location of the relevant point may instead be identified by an [[#Chevron_pointe_en_haut|upward-pointing chevron]] ('''Chevron pointe en haut''').

[[File:Chevron_bas.svg|60px]] 

* {{Tag|railway:signal:minor|FR:chevron}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:minor:form|sign}}

Optional tags:
* {{Tag|railway:signal:minor:type|down}} ('''down''' by default)

====Fixed speed indicators====

=====Fixed Ordinary-type TIV-D=====

Fixed distant speed indicators of the ordinary type ('''TIV fixes à distance du type ordinaire''') are square-shaped when indicating a speed reduction of less than 40 km/h, and diamond-shaped otherwise.

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center"
! Square !! Diamond-shape
|-
| [[File:TIV_Distance_80.svg|60px]] || [[File:TIV_Distance_Crocodile_40.svg|60px]]
|}

* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant|FR:speed_indicator}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant:fixed|FR:TIV-D}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant:fixed:form|sign}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant:fixed:speed|}} (speed as displayed on the signal, e.g. 80)

Optional tags:
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant:fixed:shape|diamond}} (default is ''square'')


The '''Z''' sign is the execution signal indicating the start of the section to be traveled at limited speed.

[[File:Tableau_Z.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit|FR:marker}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit:entry|FR:Z}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit:entry:form|sign}}


The '''R''' sign indicates the end of the section where the speed limit is required and the return to normal speed ('''Reprise de vitesse''').

[[File:Tableau_R.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit|FR:marker}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit:exit|FR:R}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit:exit:form|sign}}

=====Fixed B-type TIV-D=====

B-type fixed distant speed indicators ('''TIV fixes à distance du type B''') apply to trains authorized to travel at speeds greater than 140 km/h. When only a B-type indicator is present, the speed limit also applies to self-propelled trains (automoteurs).

[[File:FR-TIV-type-B-160.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant|FR:speed_indicator}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant:fast|FR:TIV-D}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant:fast:type|FR:B}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant:fast:form|sign}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant:fast:speed|}}

=====Fixed C-type TIV-D=====

C-type fixed distant speed indicators ('''TIV fixes à distance du type C''') specifically apply to railcars and self-propelled trains (autorails et automoteurs).

[[File:FR_TIV-type-C-180.svg|60px]]

When a B-type and a C-type fixed distant speed indicator are paired, the speed limit shown by the B-type indicator applies only to trains authorized to exceed 140 km/h. The C-type indicator serves as a reminder of the upstream speed limit for railcars and self-propelled trains.

If the speed limit differs between pendular trains and other self-propelled trains, the C-type indicator displays two numbers. The lower section, in italics and showing the higher value, indicates the limit for pendular trains; the upper section shows the limit for other self-propelled units.

* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant|FR:speed_indicator}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant:railcar|FR:TIV-D}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant:railcar:type|FR:C}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant:railcar:form|sign}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant:railcar:speed|}} (separated by <code>;</code> if more than one speed limit has to be specified)

====Speed limit announcement (P board)====

The P board announces a distant speed indicator (TIV-D) for lines equipped with pre-announcement signalling. This board applies only to trains running at speeds higher than 160 km/h and requires the train to pass the next TIV-D at a speed lower than 160 km/h. This sign is lit at night.

[[File:FR_Tableau_P.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant|FR:P}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant:form||({{TagValue|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant:form|sign}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant:form|light}})}} (board/light box)

====Pentagonal speed indicators====

Pentagonal TIVs typically concern freight trains and possibly certain locomotive series. The TIV figure specifies the maximum speed in tens of kilometers per hour (e.g. 5 stands for 50 km/h).

=====Pentagonal distant TIV=====

A distant upward-pointing pentagonal speed indicator (TIV-D) announces a special speed restriction. 

[[File:TIV_Pentagonal_Distant.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant|FR:speed_indicator}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant:freight|FR:TIV-D}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant:freight:form|sign}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant:freight:shape|pentagon}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant:freight:speed|}}

======L plate======

When the restriction applies only to certain locomotive series, the pentagonal TIV-D is accompanied by an '''L''' plate placed below it.

[[File:Plaque_L.svg|40px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant|FR:speed_indicator}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant:freight:condition|FR:L}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant:freight:condition:form|sign}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit_distant:freight:condition:for|locomotive}}


=====Pentagonal TIV=====

A downward-pointing pentagonal TIV, with a white digit on a black background, marks the beginning of the speed-restricted section and indicates that the speed limit is now in effect.

[[File:TIV_Pentagonal_Exécution.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit|FR:speed_indicator}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit:freight|FR:TIV-E}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit:freight:form|sign}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit:freight:shape|pentagon}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit:freight:function|entry}} 
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit:freight:speed|}}

=====Pentagonal white board=====

A downward-pointing pentagonal white board ('''Tableau blanc''' in French) without any markings indicates the end of the track section to be traversed at a limited speed.

[[File:TIV_Pentagonal_Reprise.svg|60px]] 

* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit|FR:speed_indicator}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit:freight|FR:white_board}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit:freight:form|sign}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit:freight:shape|pentagon}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit:freight:function|exit}}

====Speed marker (Km sign)====

A speed marker or '''Repère de Vitesse''' is used in conjunction with TIVs to indicate transition points of the speed limit when these do not coincide with notable landmarks.

[[File:FR_Pancarte_Km.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit|FR:marker}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit:transition|FR:Km}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:speed_limit:transition:form|sign}}

===Route indicators===

Routes are signaled with either an '''Indicateur de Direction''' (ID) or a '''Tableau Indicateur de Direction à Distance''' (TIDD), or both. 

====Indicateur de Direction====

An '''Indicateur de Direction''' (ID) is typically grouped with the [[#Carré|Carré]] signal preceding the relevant facing point(s).

[[File:Indicateur_de_Direction_(ID3).svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:route|FR:ID}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:route:form|light}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:route:states||({{TagValue|railway:signal:route:states|FR:ID1}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:route:states|FR:ID2}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:route:states|FR:ID3}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:route:states|FR:ID4}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:route:states|FR:ID5}})}}

====Tableau Indicateur de Direction à Distance====

The '''Tableau Indicateur de Direction à Distance''' (TIDD) indicates the position of the next point, either left or right unless the next signal displays a closed [[#Carré|Carré]] in which case, the TIDD is off.

[[File:TIDD_gauche.svg|60px]]
[[File:TIDD_droit.svg|60px]] 

* {{Tag|railway:signal:switch|FR:TIDD}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:switch:form|light}}

====Tableau Lumineux de Direction====

For some routes, a '''Tableau Lumineux de Direction''' (TLD) provides a more precise indication of the direction, in the form of a track number, an abbreviation, or a destination name.

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center"
! Wide box !! Double box
|-
| [[File:TLD_box.svg|120px]]|| [[File:TLD_V2_box.svg|60px]]
|}

* {{Tag|railway:signal:route_distant|FR:TLD}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:route_distant:form|light}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:route_distant:shape||({{TagValue|railway:signal:route_distant:shape|wide}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:route_distant:shape|double}})}}

Optional tags:
* {{Tag|railway:signal:route_distant:caption|}}  (information shown when the signal is lit)

====Track indicator sign====

[[File:Pancarte nom de voie.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:route_info|FR:track_name}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:route_info:form|sign}}

Optional tags:
* {{Tag|railway:signal:route_info:caption|}} (text as shown on the sign)

====Branch lines====

The '''BIF''' board announces a trailing switch junction with multiple tracks.

[[File:Tableau_BIF.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:route_distant|FR:BIF}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:route_distant:form|sign}}


The '''Y''' board announces a facing switch junction.

[[File:Tableau_Y.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:route_distant|FR:Y}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:route_distant:form|sign}}

===Wrong-road signalling===

These boards are used on double-track sections equipped with wrong-road signalling installations, also known in French as '''Installations Permanentes de ContreSens''' (IPCS).

The wrong-road entry board or '''Tableau d'Entrée de ContreSens''' (TECS) indicates or confirms to the driver that the train is authorised to run in the opposite direction to normal traffic.

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center"
!Board (sign)!!Box (light)
|-
|[[File:Tableau_d'entrée_de_contresens_(TECS).svg|60px]]||[[File:TECS_box.svg|60px]]
|}

* {{Tag|railway:signal:wrong_road|FR:transition}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:wrong_road:entry|FR:TECS}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:wrong_road:entry:form||({{TagValue|railway:signal:wrong_road:entry:form|sign}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:wrong_road:entry:form|light}})}}


The wrong-road exit board or '''Tableau de Sortie de ContreSens''' (TSCS) informs the driver that the train, currently running in the wrong road, is now directed back to the normal running direction.

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center"
!Board (sign)!!Box (light)
|-
|[[File:Tableau_de_sortie_de_contresens_(TSCS).svg|60px]]||[[File:TSCS_box.svg|60px]]
|}

* {{Tag|railway:signal:wrong_road|FR:transition}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:wrong_road:exit|FR:TSCS}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:wrong_road:exit:form||({{TagValue|railway:signal:wrong_road:exit:form|sign}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:wrong_road:exit:form|light}})}}

===Cab signalling===

To allow trains to run safely at speeds above 220 km/h, traditional lineside signals are replaced by in-cab signalling systems. These systems transmit movement authorities and speed instructions directly to the driver's cab.

In France, the two main cab signalling systems are [https://wikipedia.org/wiki/Transmission_Voie-Machine Transmission Voie-Machine (TVM)] and [https://wikipedia.org/wiki/European_Train_Control_System the European Train Control System (ETCS)].

=====CAB boards=====

The train is directed towards a cab signalling line. One or more boards of this type precede the entry.

[[File:Signalisation_de_Cabine_Distant.svg|60px]] 

* {{Tag|railway:signal:train_protection|FR:cab_signalling_advance}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:train_protection:form|sign}}


The train enters a cab signalling line. This board is at the position of the last signal or immediately downstream of that signal.

[[File:Signalisation_de_Cabine.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:train_protection|FR:cab_signalling_entry}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:train_protection:form|sign}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:train_protection:function|entry}}


The train is returning to a traditional lateral signalling line.

[[File:Fin_de_Signalisation_de_Cabine.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:train_protection|FR:cab_signalling_exit}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:train_protection:form|sign}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:train_protection:function|exit}}

=====Markers=====

======TVM stop marker======

[[File:Repère_TGV.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:train_protection|FR:marker}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:train_protection:main|stop_marker}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:train_protection:main:form|sign}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:train_protection:main:plate||({{TagValue|railway:signal:train_protection:main:plate|FR:F}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:train_protection:main:plate|FR:NF}})}} (identification plate)

Optional tags:
* {{Tag|railway:signal:train_protection:main:passing_light|yes}} (if '''Nf''' signal is equipped with a white passing light)

======Shunting marker======

A shunting marker (French: '''Jalon de Manœuvre''') is placed at a point where certain train movements are executed. These movements may include reversing, shunting, or departing trains from a work site. The white chevron points to the track involved.

[[File:Jalon_Manoeuvre.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:shunting|FR:marker}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:shunting:form|sign}}

Optional tags:
* {{Tag|railway:signal:shunting:passing_light|yes}} (if equipped with a white passing light)

===Stations and facilities===

====Stations====

- The '''Gare''' sign indicates the approach to a facility where special regulations for stations apply. Typically, a [[#Chevron_pointe_en_bas|downward-pointing chevron]] placed in front of the entry point marks the entrance to the facility.

[[File:Pancarte_GARE.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:station_distant|FR:GARE}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:station_distant:form|sign}}


- This marker is placed at the track entrance in certain stations on single-track lines to remind drivers that exit signals are exceptionally installed on both sides of the passing tracks.

[[File:Repère SID.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:station|FR:bilateral_exit_signals}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:station:form|sign}}

====Facilities====

In this context, ''facility'' refers to any non-station railway site (French: ''établissement'') such as a depot, yard, or technical installation.

- This sign marks the approach to certain railway facilities.

[[File:Schéma_de_signalisation_ferroviaire_Pancarte_d'annonce.svg|120px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:station_distant|FR:facility_approach}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:station_distant:form|sign}}

Optional tags:
* {{Tag|railway:signal:station_distant:caption|}} (text as show on the sign, e.g. ''Chailles à 200m'')


- This sign marks the approach to a facility on ''single-track lines with simplified signalling'' (VUSS), indicating that the upcoming points must not be passed at a speed exceeding 30 km/h (40 km/h on certain lines).

[[File:Approche_Etablissement.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:station_distant|FR:facility_approach_simplified}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:station_distant:form|sign}}


- The boundary of certain facilities are marked by the diamond-shaped sign below:

[[File:LIMITETS.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:station|FR:facility_boundary}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:station:form|sign}}


====Stops====

[[File:Pancarte_Arrêt_A.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop_distant|FR:ARRET_A}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop_distant:form|sign}}

Optional tags:
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop_distant:caption|}} (text as shown on the sign)


[[File:Pancarte_ARRET.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop|FR:ARRET}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop:form|sign}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop:arrangement||({{TagValue|railway:signal:stop:arrangement|horizontal}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:stop:arrangement|vertical}})}}


[[File:Pancarte_ATC.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop|FR:ATC}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop:form|sign}}


[[File:Pancarte_STOP_A.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop_distant|FR:STOP_A}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop_distant:form|sign}}

Optional tags:
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop_distant:caption|}} (text as shown on the sign)


[[File:Pancarte_STOP.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop|FR:STOP}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop:form|sign}}


- The <span id="StopMarker">'''Jalon d'arrêt à damier rouge et blanc'''</span> (red and white checkered stop marker) is similar to the [[#Carré|Carré]] signal; it commands a stop before the point where it is placed and cannot be crossed.

[[File:Jalon_d'arret.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop|FR:marker}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop:type|stop_marker}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop:form|sign}}

====Passenger stops====

Signs used to mark the stopping point for passenger trains.

- Stopping point for the front of the train (Tête de Train), regardless of the number of vehicles in the train.

[[File:ARRET_VOY_TT.svg|60px]]  

* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop|FR:passenger_stop}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop:classic|FR:TT}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop:classic:form|sign}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop:classic:for|head_of_train}}


- Stopping point for trains equipped for driver-only operations, referred to as '''Équipé Agent Seul''' (EAS).

[[File:ARRET_VOY_TT_EAS.svg|60px]]  

* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop|FR:passenger_stop}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop:classic|FR:TT_EAS}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop:classic:form|sign}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop:classic:for|driver_only}}



- Stopping point for the front of the train (Tête de Train), regardless of the number of vehicles in the train, including the locomotive.

[[File:ARRET_VOY_TTL.svg|60px]]  

* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop|FR:passenger_stop}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop:classic|FR:TTL}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop:classic:form|sign}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop:classic:for|locomotive}}


- Stopping point for trains composed of the number of carriages indicated on the sign.

[[File:ARRET_VOY_6V.svg|60px]]
[[File:ARRET_VOY_12V.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop|FR:passenger_stop}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop:classic|FR:vehicle_number}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop:classic:form|sign}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop:classic:for|vehicles}}

Optional tags:
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop:classic:caption|}} (text as shown on the sign, e.g. ''6V'', ''12V'')


- Stopping point for TGVs. The number indicates the number of rakes: 1=single unit, 2=multiple units.

[[File:ARRET_VOY_TGV1.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop|FR:passenger_stop}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop:highspeed|FR:TGV1}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop:highspeed:form|sign}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop:highspeed:for|single_unit}}


[[File:ARRET_VOY_TGV2.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop|FR:passenger_stop}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop:highspeed|FR:TGV2}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop:highspeed:form|sign}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop:highspeed:for|multiple_unit}}


[[File:ARRET_VOY_TGV1-2.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop|FR:passenger_stop}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop:highspeed|FR:TGV1-2}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:stop:highspeed:form|sign}}

====Train departure====

=====Reporting Plate=====

In general, the driver of a train stopped by a [[#Carré|Carré]], a [[#Carré_violet|Carré violet]], or a [[#Guidon_d'arrêt|Guidon d'arrêt]] must report to the signaller if the signal does not clear immediately.

If a signal is equipped with a telephone that is not located in its immediate vicinity, its location may be indicated by a reporting plate ('''Plaque de Reconnaissance''' in French).

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center"
!Default!!Delayed!!Exemption
|-
|[[File:Plaque de reconnaissance avec VAT.svg|60px]]
||[[File:Plaque de reconnaissance différée avec VAT.svg|60px]]
||[[File:Plaque de dispense de reconnaissance avec VAT.svg|60px]]
|}

* {{Tag|railway:signal:departure|FR:reporting}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:departure:type||({{TagValue|railway:signal:departure:type|standard}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:departure:type|delayed}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:departure:type|exempt}})}} (''standard'' is the optional default value)
* {{Tag|railway:signal:departure:form|sign}}

Optional tags:

- If the plate is equipped with a telephone call indicator (French: '''Voyant d'Appel Téléphonique'''):
* {{Tag|railway:signal:departure:phone:indicator|yes}}

- If an arrow indicates the direction to the telephone:
* {{Tag|railway:signal:departure:phone:direction||({{TagValue|railway:signal:departure:phone:direction|left}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:departure:phone:direction|right}})}}

=====Departure request=====

Some [[#Carré|Carré]] signals are equipped with a '''Demande de Départ''' (DD) plate. The driver waiting for departure must, one minute before the scheduled departure time or if they notice the corresponding Carré signal has opened, request departure authorization by telephone.

[[File:Plaque_DD.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:departure|FR:DD}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:departure:type|request}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:departure:form|sign}}

=====Departure authorization=====

On certain tracks, authorization for departure may be given to the driver by a flashing half-white, half-green '''Signal Lumineux de Départ''' (SLD), also known as a '''Signal Lumineux d'Autorisation de Mouvement'''.

[[File:SLD_box.gif|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:departure|FR:SLD}} 
* {{Tag|railway:signal:departure:type|await}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:departure:form|light}}


Alternatively, departure authorization may be given simply by the opening of the corresponding [[#Carré|Carré]] signal; these Carré signals are identified by a half-white, half-green plate.

[[File:Plaque_mi-blanche_mi-verte.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:departure|FR:departure_marker}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:departure:type|allow}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:departure:form|sign}}

=====Répétiteur Lumineux d'Itinéraire=====

A '''Répétiteur Lumineux d'Itinéraire''' (RLI), a route light repeater, is a board providing route information to the staff member responsible for train departure.

[[File:RLI_box.svg|120px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:departure|FR:RLI}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:departure:form|light}}

Optional tags:
* {{Tag|railway:signal:departure:caption|}} (text shown on the display)

===Converging tracks===

====Chevron pointe en haut====

Upward-pointing chevrons indicate either an exit from a junction of converging tracks or, in some stations, the point beyond which trains must not proceed when a stop is required.

[[File:Chevron_haut.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:minor|FR:chevron}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:minor:type|up}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:minor:form|sign}}

====Tableau Lumineux de Correspondance====

A '''Tableau Lumineux de Correspondance''' (TLC) for converging tracks is a signal installed to the left of the track it addresses and is normally off. At any given time, it can display one of the following:

- A white letter '''T''', either ''fixed'' if the group signal is open or ''flashing'', if the group signal is closed.

[[File:TLC-T.svg|70px]]

- The [[#Signal Lumineux de Départ|Signal Lumineux de Départ]] if applicable.

[[File:TLC-SLD.gif|70px]]

- A red and white checkerboard, which has the same meaning as the [[#StopMarker|Jalon d'Arrêt]] and is used instead of the [[#Chevron_pointe_en_haut|chevron]].

[[File:TLC-C.svg|70px]]


* {{Tag|railway:signal:minor|FR:TLC}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:minor:form|light}}

====Tableau Indicateur de Provenance====

At the exit of some service track yards, a '''Tableau Indicateur de Provenance''' (TIP), a track indicator, complements the [[#Chevron_pointe_en_haut|chevron]]. It can display any of the numbers designating each of the tracks. When the TIP displays a number, the driver on the corresponding track is allowed to cross the chevron. When the group signal is closed, the driver can advance to an agreed point, usually in preparation for a reversing movement.

[[File:TIP_box.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:minor|FR:TIP}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:minor:form|light}}

===Service tracks and shunting===

====Signal Lumineux de Manœuvre====

A '''Signal Lumineux de Manœuvre''' (SLM) is installed in some installations to facilitate shunting operations on main tracks (for example, pushing a train into a siding) or on service tracks. It consists of three white lights, which are normally unlit. Several similar signals may be installed along the same track to mark the full extent of the shunting area.

[[File:SLM_all.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:shunting|FR:SLM}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:shunting:form|light}}

====Shunting boards====

The '''D''' (Depot) board indicates that the train is directed towards depot tracks.

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center"
!Mechanical (sign)!!Luminous (light)
|-
|[[File:Tableau_D.svg|60px]]||[[File:Tableau_D_box.svg|60px]]
|}

* {{Tag|railway:signal:shunting_route|FR:destination}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:shunting_route:depot|FR:D}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:shunting_route:depot:form||
({{TagValue|railway:signal:shunting_route:depot:form|sign}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:shunting_route:depot:form|light}})}} (sign=mechanical; light=luminous)


The '''G''' (Garage) board indicates that the train is directed towards a service track.

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center"
!Mechanical (sign)!!Luminous (light)
|-
|[[File:Tableau_G.svg|60px]]||[[File:Tableau_G_box.svg|60px]]
|}

* {{Tag|railway:signal:shunting_route|FR:destination}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:shunting_route:stabling|FR:G}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:shunting_route:stabling:form||
({{TagValue|railway:signal:shunting_route:stabling:form|sign}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:shunting_route:stabling:form|light}})}} (sign=mechanical; light=luminous)


The '''Heurtoir à xxx mètres''' board announces a buffer stop at the specified distance.

[[File:Heurtoir_500m.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:shunting_route|FR:destination}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:shunting_route:buffer_stop|FR:Heurtoir}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:shunting_route:buffer_stop:form|sign}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:shunting_route:buffer_stop:caption|*}} (text as show on the sign, e.g. "Heurtoir à 500m")


The '''Imp''' (Impasse) board indicates that the train is directed towards a dead-end track.

[[File:Tableau_Imp.svg|60px]]  

* {{Tag|railway:signal:shunting_route|FR:destination}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:shunting_route:dead_end|FR:Imp}} 
* {{Tag|railway:signal:shunting_route:dead_end:form||
({{TagValue|railway:signal:shunting_route:dead_end:form|sign}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:shunting_route:dead_end:form|light}})}} (sign=mechanical; light=luminous)

The '''SAS''' board indicates that the train is directed towards a generally very short section of track (sas) with [[#Carré|Carré]] signals at each end.

[[File:Pancarte_SAS.svg|60px]] 

* {{Tag|railway:signal:shunting_route|FR:destination}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:shunting_route:headshunt|FR:SAS}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:shunting_route:headshunt:form||
({{TagValue|railway:signal:shunting_route:headshunt:form|sign}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:shunting_route:headshunt:form|light}})}} (sign=mechanical; light=luminous)

====Shunting signs====

The '''LGR''' (Limite pour Garage par Refoulement) sign.

[[File:Pancarte_LGR.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:shunting|FR:LGR}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:shunting:form|sign}}


'''LM''' (Limite de Manœuvre) sign.

[[File:Tableau_LM.svg|60px]] 

* {{Tag|railway:signal:shunting|FR:LM}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:shunting:form|sign}}


'''MV''' (Marche à Vue) sign.

[[File:Pancarte_MV.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:shunting|FR:MV}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:shunting:form|sign}}

===Electrified railway lines===

====Neutral sections====

A neutral section (French: ''zone de sectionnement'') is a grounded catenary section, isolated on both sides, used to separate portions of track either powered by different substations or phases, or when switching voltages from 1500 V DC to 25 kV AC and vice versa, or to accommodate different voltages and frequencies in cross-border areas.

=====Power-off advance signal=====

The advance board indicates the presence of a neutral section ahead, located at the specified distance (in meters).

[[File:Schéma_de_signalisation_ferroviaire_Pancarte_"Sectionnement".svg|60px]] 

* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity|FR:power_off_advance}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity:type|power_off_advance}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity:form|sign}}

Optional tags:
* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity:caption|}} (text as shown on the sign)

=====Power-off signal=====

The power-off (French: ''Coupez courant'') board requires the main circuit breaker to be opened when passing through a neutral section, which it traverses using its own kinetic energy (''marche sur l'erre'').

[[File:Couper_Courant.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity|FR:power_off}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity:type|power_off}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity:form||
({{TagValue|railway:signal:electricity:form|sign}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:electricity:form|light}})}} (sign=mechanical; light=luminous)

=====Power-on signal=====

This board indicates the end of the neutral section.

[[File:Fin_Coupez_Courant.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity|FR:power_on}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity:type|power_on}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity:form||
({{TagValue|railway:signal:electricity:form|sign}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:electricity:form|light}})}} (sign=mechanical; light=luminous)

Optional tags:
* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity:voltage|}} (voltage of the current line; usually 1500 V or 25 kV)
* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity:frequency|}} (frequency of the current; 50 Hz for 25 kV; 0 for 1500 V DC)

=====REV sign=====

This sign indicates the end of the neutral section for reversible trains or trains with multiple pantographs.

[[File:Pancarte_REV.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity|FR:REV}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity:type|power_on}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity:form|sign}}

Optional tags:
* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity:voltage|}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity:frequency|}}

====Pantograph down====

=====Pantograph down advance signal=====

The pantograph down (French: ''Baissez panto'') advance signal instructs the lowering of the train's pantograph(s) under specific circumstances:

* '''Transition from 1500 V DC to 25 kV AC''': Required when switching from direct current (DC) to alternating current (AC) power supply.
* '''Between Two 25 kV Substations''': Necessary due to phase shift, potentially creating voltage differences of up to 70 kV in case of opposite phases.
* '''Phase Change''': Used to redistribute the electrical load within the network.
* '''Cross-border Areas''': Needed when crossing borders where different electrical systems may be in operation.
* '''Crossing Certain Structures''': Specific structures may require the pantograph to be lowered for safety or operational reasons.


[[File:Baissez_Panto_distant.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity|FR:pantograph_down_advance}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity:type|pantograph_down_advance}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity:form||
({{TagValue|railway:signal:electricity:form|sign}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:electricity:form|light}})}} (sign=mechanical; light=luminous)

=====Pantograph down signal=====

This board indicates the point where pantographs must be lowered.

[[File:Baisser_Panto.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity|FR:pantograph_down}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity:type|pantograph_down}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity:form||
({{TagValue|railway:signal:electricity:form|sign}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:electricity:form|light}})}} (sign=mechanical; light=luminous)

Optional tags:
* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity:voltage|}}

=====Pantograph up signal=====

This board marks the end of the catenary section requiring pantographs to be lowered. Pantographs may be raised after passing the signal.

[[File:Fin_Baisser_Panto.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity|FR:pantograph_up}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity:type|pantograph_up}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity:form||
({{TagValue|railway:signal:electricity:form|sign}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:electricity:form|light}})}} (sign=mechanical; light=luminous)

Optional tags:
* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity:voltage|}}

====Dual-mode traffic====

This distant sign announces traffic towards a non-electrified line. This sign accompanies the pantograph down (''Baissez panto'') distant and execution signals when they apply only to dual-mode traffic. In this case, they are not followed by a [[#Pantograph down end signal|Pantograph down end signal]].

[[File:Pancarte_BIMODE_A.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:dual_mode|FR:BIMODE_A}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:dual_mode:form|sign}}

Optional tags:
* {{Tag|railway:signal:dual_mode:caption|}} (text as shown on the sign)


This distant signal announces traffic coming from a non-electrified line for dual-mode trains only. This sign accompanies the [[#Pantograph down end signal|Pantograph down end signal]] when it applies only to dual-mode traffic. It is not preceded by the '''Baissez Panto''' (Pantograph Down) distant and execution signals. 

[[File:Pancarte_BIMODE.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:dual_mode|FR:BIMODE}} 
* {{Tag|railway:signal:dual_mode:form|sign}}

====End of catenary====

Beyond this sign, the line is no longer electrified and only thermal or dual-mode trains can continue.

[[File:FIN_CAT.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity|FR:end_of_catenary}} 
* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity:type|end_of_catenary}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity:form|sign}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:position|overhead}}

====Frost board====

'''GIVRE''' (frost) boards are installed on some 1500 V DC electrified lines. When a driver encounters such an illuminated board, the second pantograph must be raised to improve current collection: the first pantograph clears ice from the catenary, improving contact for the second one.

The second pantograph is lowered when another frost board in the off state is encountered, after verbal authorization, or when no icing conditions are observed.

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center"
!Board (sign)!!Box (light)
|-
|[[File:Tableau_GIVRE.svg|60px]]||[[File:Tableau_GIVRE_box.svg|60px]]
|}

* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity|FR:frost}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:electricity:form||({{TagValue|railway:signal:electricity:form|sign}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:electricity:form|light}})}}

===ETCS signals===

See [[OpenRailwayMap/ETCS_Markers|ETCS markers]].

===Level crossings===

====Level crossing announcement====

This sign informs the driver that they are approaching an unguarded level crossing where they must stop in accordance with specific operating instructions.

[[File:PN.svg|60px]] 

* {{Tag|railway:signal:crossing_hint|FR:PN_A}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:crossing_hint:form|sign}}

Optional tags:
* {{Tag|railway:signal:crossing_hint:caption|}} (text as shown on the sign)

====Level crossing ahead====

This sign indicates the ''operating mode'' of the level crossing:

* '''Automatic mode''' (no white band): closure is triggered automatically when a section of track — starting about 20 meters <!-- cf. IN 1482 V1 --> before the crossing — is occupied.

* '''Manual mode''' (white band): closure or operation is carried out by the train crew.

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center"
! Automatic mode !! Manual mode
|-
| [[File:Pancarte_PN.svg|60px]] || [[File:Pancarte_PN_avec_bandeau.svg|60px]]
|}

* {{Tag|railway:signal:crossing_info|FR:PN}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:crossing_info:form|sign}}

Optional tags:
* {{Tag|railway:signal:crossing_info:caption|}} (text as show on the sign)
* {{Tag|railway:signal:crossing_info:automatic|no}} (indicates a manual level crossing — default is automatic)

====Conditional crossing====

Level crossings equipped with automatic light and sound signalling (SAL) may also be fitted with a '''Signal lumineux longitudinal dit de "Franchissement Conditionnel"''' (SFC), a "conditional crossing" light signal. This signal consists of one or two green lights ("feux verts" in French) arranged vertically or horizontally.

The SFC signal informs the train driver of the state of the level crossing, ensuring that, in case of system failure, the train can be stopped before the crossing. The level crossing may only be passed without stopping when the SFC signal is lit, confirming its normal closed state. An unlit SFC signal is equivalent to a stop indication.

[[File:Feux_Verts.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:crossing|FR:SFC}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:crossing:form|light}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:crossing:arrangement||({{TagValue|railway:signal:crossing:arrangement|single}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:crossing:arrangement|horizontal}}/{{TagValue|railway:signal:crossing:arrangement|vertical}})}}

===Whistle===

The '''S''' sign indicates locations where the driver must sound the horn, such as when approaching unguarded level crossings, entering tunnels, or passing through areas with reduced visibility.

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center"
! S sign !! S sign with J plate
|-
|[[File:Pancarte_S.svg|60px]]||[[File:Pancarte_S+J.svg|60px]]
|}

* {{Tag|railway:signal:whistle|FR:S}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:whistle:form|sign}}

Optional tags:

When a '''J''' plate is present below the '''S''' sign, it indicates that the whistling obligation applies only during ''daytime'' (between 07:00 and 20:00).

* {{Tag|railway:signal:whistle:plate|FR:J}}

===Miscellaneous signs===

This signal marks the approach to a permanent or temporary reduced clearance.

[[File:Pancarte_Gabarit.svg|60px]]

* {{Tag|railway:signal:minor|FR:reduced_clearance}}
* {{Tag|railway:signal:minor:form|sign}}

= Sources =

*[https://www.securite-ferroviaire.fr/sites/default/files/reglementations/pdf/2023-03/document-pedagogique-signaux-regimes-exploitation-v1.pdf Les signaux – Les régimes d’exploitation des lignes – Les systèmes d’espacement des trains] (EPSF, 2023, PDF)
*[https://www.securite-ferroviaire.fr/sites/default/files/reglementations/pdf/2023-03/rfn-ig-se-01-00-num-012-v3.pdf Signalisation au sol et signalisation à main – Document d'exploitation] (SNCF Réseau, 2023, PDF)
*[https://www.jonroma.net/media/rail/opdocs/world/france/S%201%20A%20I-%20Signalisation%20au%20sol.pdf Signalisation au sol] (SNCF, 2005, PDF)
*[https://www.jonroma.net/media/rail/opdocs/world/france/S%201%20B%20I%E2%80%93%20Arre%CC%82t%20d%27un%20train%20par%20un%20signal.pdf Arrêt d’un train par un signal carré, un guidon d’arrêt ou un sémaphore fermé] (SNCF, 2005, PDF)
*[https://wikipedia.org/wiki/French_railway_signalling/ French railway signalling]
*[https://ressources.data.sncf.com/explore/dataset/signalisation-permanente/information/ Signalisation permanente] (SNCF)
*[https://ressources.data.sncf.com/explore/dataset/lexique-des-acronymes-sncf/information/ Lexique des acronymes] (SNCF)
*[https://github.com/nicolaswurtz/signalisation-rfn-svg signalisation-rfn-svg] (Nicolas Wurtz, SVG)
*[https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Category:Diagrams_of_railway_signals_in_France Diagrams of railway signals in France] (Various authors, SVG+GIF)


{{Railway signals}}
